privileged_info_summary: The task operates on the real eCRM-001 conceptual aircraft model. Verified that the expected output paths in metadata explicitly match the task instructions and export scripts. However, discovered two critical defects making the task programmatically unscorable: 1) The `export_result.sh` script parses the `.vsp3` XML looking for `<WingSect>` tags, but OpenVSP v3/v5 format uses `<XSec>` tags. This causes the script to always find 0 sections, perpetually failing the verifier's `fold_sections > base_sections` check and outer dihedral angle check. 2) The verifier and prompt assume the baseline 'Wing' has a ~58m span and expect the folded span report to fall strictly between 30.0 and 55.0. In reality, the component named 'Wing' in the provided `.vsp3` file has a `TotalSpan` of 454.34 units. Since the agent is explicitly instructed to fold the 'Wing' component, any accurate mathematical span reduction report based on the 454.34 unit 'Wing' will fail the strict [30.0, 55.0] verifier constraint.
